title: Mustardy Gooey Uruguayan Reblochon Empanadas
description: Uruguayan empanadas filled with a rich reblochon cheese and mustard mixture, steamed to perfection. This diabetic-friendly recipe serves as a satisfying solo dinner.

ingredients.name: Reblochon cheese
ingredients.notes: Pasturized cow cheese originating from the French Alps.

ingredients.name: Dijon mustard
ingredients.notes: Fermented mustard made from brown or black mustard seeds.

ingredients.name: Fresh parsley
ingredients.notes: Herb often used as a garnish or added to various dishes for flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Crystalline mineral used as a seasoning.

ingredients.name: Pastry dough
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought dough used for pastry.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Large egg used for egg wash.

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: Purified water for steaming the empanadas.

instructions: Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of 1/8 inch. Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a small bowl, mix together the reblochon cheese, Dijon mustard, parsley, salt, and black pepper. Spoon a small amount of the cheese mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada. Use a fork to crimp the edges and prevent the filling from escaping during steaming.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Place the empanadas in a steamer basket and steam over boiling water for 12-15 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown and the cheese is melted and gooey.
